mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS rises > 100k in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) signals weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both HOUST and RSAFS decline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates rising supply-side capacity utilization, which pressures profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO rises > 1.5% in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank rate hikes, which increase discount rates and depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate profitability, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening economic demand, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish monetary policy bias (FEDFUNDS below 1%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL above 2%) creates a 'stagflation' environment that depresses equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL > 2%.
